author: runner-1
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9: a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder completed by ops amendment after the judge-m1p run and before the final-phase record; no design field is touched). The judge-m1p blind STAND-IN run (GPT-5.6-Sol via npx-pinned @openai/codex 0.144.1, reasoning effort low, isolated CODEX_HOME, subscription auth) completed all 300 annotation-sample items in seed-pinned real_order (per-stratum blind renderings: concept/prime glosses, abstain candidate lists, none 119-entry inventory), then 40 deranged-gloss probes, then 30 retest duplicates (preflight PASS on both calibration items first-attempt; 0 no-label across all strata; 0 transport retries; every per-call blinding grep clean; final blinding sweep CLEAN). data/m0a-judgments-llmproxy/ was assembled: judge-m1p-responses.jsonl (300), judge-m1p-probe-responses.jsonl (40), judge-m1p-retest-responses.jsonl (30), labels-proxy.jsonl (300; per-stratum labels + agent-comparator diagnostic), rendered-prompt-manifest.jsonl (370 byte-exactness witnesses), and summary.json (the exact analysis-input integers per analysis/m0a_llmproxy.py). This ops amendment resolves the placeholder in pins.corpus_hashes to the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest of data/m0a-judgments-llmproxy/ (tools/registry/corpus-pin.py m0a-judgments-llmproxy). One-shot pin discipline: any change to those bytes before the final-phase run invalidates this value and requires a pre-run correction record, not a new ops amendment.
